vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1395: Ctrl+K palette: semantic conversation search with excerpts (Phase 2)

plan.title: Ctrl+K palette: semantic conversation search with excerpts (Phase 2)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Phase 1 wired commands, memory FTS, observations, and issues into the Ctrl+K palette but left conversations out. Conversation transcripts live as JSONL session files and are not keyword-or-semantically searchable from the palette. The user wants to search conversations semantically and see an excerpt that points at the matching message.
narratives.Proposal: Build a conversation semantic-search subsystem: a JSONL chunker, a sqlite-vec + FTS5 sidecar DB, a pluggable embedding provider (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small default), a hybrid (BM25 + vector, RRF-fused) ranker, and incremental indexing (startup scan + chokidar watcher, idempotent on byteOffset). Extend GET /api/palette/search to fill the already-reserved conversations[] with ⦇…⦈-marked excerpts. Add a Conversations group to CommandPalette that opens the conversation drawer scrolled to the matched message. Add a Settings → Conversation Search section (provider/model/key, enable toggle, last-indexed, Reindex button) with a >$1 cost guard. Feature ships OFF by default.

items.title: Add conversationSearch config block (OFF by default)

narrative.Action: Add a conversationSearch section to ~/.panopticon/config.yaml schema (src/lib/config-yaml.ts): { enabled: false, provider: 'openai', model: 'text-embedding-3-small', apiKeyRef, dbPath default ~/.panopticon/conversations/embeddings.db }. Default enabled=false. Expose a typed accessor other beads read.

subItems.title: config.yaml exposes conversationSearch with enabled defaulting to false
A typed accessor returns provider/model/dbPath/enabled; covered by a unit test

items.title: JSONL conversation chunker emitting byte-offset-keyed records

narrative.Action: Implement a streaming chunker (new lib module) that reads a JSONL session, segments by message boundary, splits long messages on ~512-token windows with overlap, and emits records { sessionId, projectId, role, ts, byteOffset, charLength, text }. byteOffset must point at the message's start in the file and remain stable across appends. This record shape is the cross-cutting contract consumed by the indexer and ranker.

subItems.title: Streams a JSONL session and emits one+ records per message with correct sessionId/projectId/role/ts
Long messages split into ~512-token overlapping windows
byteOffset/charLength accurately locate the source bytes and are stable when the file is later appended to

items.title: Embeddings sidecar DB: sqlite-vec + FTS5 schema and init

narrative.Action: Create the sidecar DB module at ~/.panopticon/conversations/embeddings.db using better-sqlite3: a chunks table (the chunker record fields + embedding), a sqlite-vec virtual table for cosine ANN, an FTS5 table over chunk text for BM25, and a per-file byteOffset cursor table for incremental idempotency. Load the sqlite-vec extension behind a single guarded init; fail closed (report unavailable) if the extension can't load rather than crashing the dashboard.

subItems.title: DB initializes with chunks, vec virtual table, FTS5 table, and per-file cursor table at the configured path
sqlite-vec extension load is isolated and failure degrades the feature to unavailable without crashing the server
Upsert keyed on (sessionId, byteOffset) is idempotent — re-inserting the same chunk does not duplicate

items.title: Pluggable embedding provider (OpenAI default) + token-cost estimator

narrative.Action: Add an embedding-provider abstraction reading the config block: default OpenAI text-embedding-3-small via @ai-sdk/openai (already a dep), batched embed of N chunk texts, and a pure estimateCost(textChunks) helper used by the cost guard. Provider is selected by config; unknown/disabled provider yields a clear unavailable error.

subItems.title: embed(texts[]) returns vectors via the configured provider, batched
estimateCost(texts[]) returns a USD estimate from token count and model price; unit-tested

items.title: Incremental indexer: scan, chunk, embed, upsert (idempotent)

narrative.Action: Orchestrate indexing: enumerate JSONL session files across project dirs, read each file's persisted byteOffset cursor, process only new/appended bytes via the chunker, embed via the provider, upsert into the DB, advance the cursor. Expose a full-reindex entry (clears cursors) and an index-one-file entry (for the watcher). Skip entirely when feature disabled.

subItems.title: Startup scan embeds only chunks past each file's stored cursor
Restarting the dashboard does not re-embed already-indexed chunks
No-ops when conversationSearch.enabled is false

items.title: Live JSONL watcher feeding the incremental indexer

narrative.Action: Add a chokidar watcher (started only when feature enabled) on the JSONL session directories; debounce change events and call the indexer's index-one-file entry for changed/added files. Stop the watcher cleanly on shutdown / when the feature is disabled at runtime.

subItems.title: Appending to a watched JSONL file triggers incremental indexing of just the new bytes
Watcher only runs while the feature is enabled and stops cleanly on shutdown

items.title: Hybrid BM25 + vector ranker fused with RRF, with ⦇…⦈ excerpts

narrative.Action: Implement query-time ranking: embed the query, get vector top-k from sqlite-vec and BM25 top-k from FTS5, fuse with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and return ranked chunk rows. Build the excerpt around the matched region using the ⦇…⦈ marker convention and produce excerptSegments {text, match}[] (reuse the memory tokenizer pattern).

subItems.title: Returns hits whose source messages need not contain the exact query words (semantic recall)
BM25 and vector lists are fused via RRF into a single ranked order
Each hit carries excerpt (⦇…⦈) and excerptSegments {text, match}[]

narrative.Action: Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/palette.ts to populate the already-reserved conversations array with { sessionId, conversationId, projectId, role, ts, displayContent, excerpt, excerptSegments, rank } from the ranker. Additive only — commands/memory/observations/issues groups are untouched. Return an empty conversations[] when the feature is disabled.

subItems.title: Response conversations[] contains the documented fields, ranked, when the feature is enabled
conversations[] is empty and existing groups are unchanged when the feature is disabled

items.title: Resolve sessionId + byteOffset to a message for open-at-message

narrative.Action: Add backend support so a (sessionId, byteOffset) pair resolves to the target message identity used by the conversation drawer (extend the conversations-by-id messages handler or add a resolve helper). The frontend uses this to scroll to the matched message.

subItems.title: Given sessionId + byteOffset, the backend returns the matching message's locator (id/index) for the drawer

narrative.Action: Render a Conversations result group in CommandPalette.tsx, ordered above Memory, using the existing excerpt-segment renderer for excerptSegments. Group is hidden when conversations[] is empty/feature disabled.

subItems.title: Typing a phrase shows a Conversations group above Memory with highlighted excerpts
No Conversations group renders when there are no conversation hits

items.title: Selecting a conversation hit opens the drawer scrolled to the message

narrative.Action: Wire the conversation result's open action (sessionId + byteOffset) to open the conversation drawer and scroll to the matching message using the open-at-msg locator.

subItems.title: Selecting a conversation result opens its drawer scrolled to the matched message

narrative.Action: Add a Conversation Search section in dashboard Settings: embedding provider/model/api-key inputs, an enable/disable toggle (OFF by default), the last-indexed timestamp, and a 'Reindex all conversations' button. Persists to the conversationSearch config block.

subItems.title: Provider/model/api-key and an enable toggle (default off) are editable and persist
Last-indexed timestamp and a Reindex-all button are shown

items.title: Reindex cost guard (>$1 requires explicit confirm)

narrative.Action: Before a full reindex, estimate token cost via provider.estimateCost and show it; require an explicit confirmation when the estimate exceeds $1 before any embedding API calls are made.

subItems.title: Estimated reindex cost is shown before the reindex runs
Reindex estimated over $1 blocks until the user explicitly confirms

items.title: Unit tests: chunker boundaries + byte-offset across appends

narrative.Action: Add unit tests for the chunker: message-boundary correctness, ~512-token window splitting/overlap, and byteOffset accuracy verified before and after the fixture file is appended to.

subItems.title: Tests assert boundary segmentation and window splitting
A test appends to the fixture and asserts byteOffset of earlier and new chunks is correct

items.title: Unit tests: FTS + vector RRF ranker

narrative.Action: Add unit tests for the ranker: RRF fusion of two known rank lists produces the expected order, and excerpt/excerptSegments are emitted with the ⦇…⦈ marker. Use a deterministic stub embedding provider.

subItems.title: RRF fusion of two seeded lists yields the expected fused order
Excerpt segments are produced with correct match flags

items.title: Integration test: palette search endpoint with a fixture session

narrative.Action: Add an integration test that indexes a fixture JSONL session (stub provider), calls GET /api/palette/search, and asserts the conversations[] shape/ranking when enabled, an empty conversations[] plus intact Phase-1 groups when disabled.

subItems.title: Enabled: endpoint returns conversation hits for the fixture with documented fields
Disabled: conversations[] empty and commands/memory/observations/issues groups unchanged (no Phase-1 regression)
